summary: Iteration 6 — Verified FEATURE 1 (admin can create other admins via /api/sub-users) + FEATURE 2 (Use logo colors button extracts dominant color from logo). Found and FIXED one CRITICAL backend bug: GET /api/auth/me returned 500 for ANY newly-created sub-user because get_current_user() did not stringify the `created_by` ObjectId field. Fix applied in /app/backend/server.py line ~96 (added `if user.get('created_by'): user['created_by'] = str(user['created_by'])`). After fix, all 9 backend pytest cases PASS and all UI assertions PASS.
action_items: RESOLVED by testing agent: /api/auth/me 500 on newly-created users (ObjectId serialization for `created_by` field). Fix is one line in server.py:get_current_user().

Minor pre-existing console errors observed on Streams page: 'Encountered two children with the same key %s /streamer/api/v3/streams/{name}' (React duplicate-key warning) and 'A component is changing an uncontrolled input to be controlled' — NOT related to this task, but worth a future cleanup.
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: server.py:get_current_user() was missing `created_by` ObjectId stringification — every newly-created user (admin/reseller/client) got 500 on /auth/me. Added one-line fix. This bug was latent for any user created via /api/sub-users.

server.py:288-331 POST /sub-users — role validation correctly checks reseller cannot create admin (403) and unknown role (400). max_streams/max_sub_users/max_concurrent_viewers default to None for admin, matching spec.
server.py:271-284 GET /sub-users — admin branch correctly excludes self via {'_id': {'$ne': ObjectId(user['id'])}} and includes role in (admin, reseller, client).
server.py:373-397 DELETE /sub-users — guards in correct order: scope → self-delete (403) → admin-by-non-admin (403) → last-admin (counts other admins after exclusion). Solid.
Resellers.jsx:91-96 conditional <option> rendering is correct; line 131 + 166 hide quotas+streams when role==='admin'.

BrandingSection.jsx:30-85 extractDominantColorFromImage — 80x80 downscale, 32-step quantization, filters near-white (min>235), near-black (max<35), grayscale (max-min<35), and transparent (a<200). Returns null when all pixels filtered (button shows error rather than crashing). Verified on real red logo: returned #F70201.
BrandingSection.jsx:158-177 pickColorsFromLogo handler — disables button when !logo_data_uri, sets autoDerive=true so hover+soft recompute via useEffect on primary change. Works as designed.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all UI assertions for both features passed)

seed_data_creation: Test admins with prefix test_admin_*@example.com are created during test runs and deleted at teardown via cleanup_admins fixture. UI-created admin is also deleted via UI flow. admin@flussonic.io remains.
context_for_next_testing_agent: FEATURE 1 (admin sub-user) and FEATURE 2 (use logo colors) are both fully verified end-to-end. test_admin_subuser.py covers role validation, login of new admin, list filtering (admin sees all-non-self), self-delete guard, admin-cross-delete, reseller-cannot-delete-admin. For logo color test, the GRUPOAMIXHN logo currently in branding is red — extractDominantColorFromImage returns #F70201. The /auth/me ObjectId(created_by) bug was fixed by stringifying created_by in get_current_user. Future regression tests should call /auth/me as a freshly-created user.
rca of the issue: RCA — /api/auth/me 500 for newly-created users: get_current_user() in server.py loaded the user document from MongoDB and stringified _id and parent_id, but the `created_by` field (added when /api/sub-users creates a user, storing the creator's ObjectId) was left as a raw bson.ObjectId. When FastAPI's jsonable_encoder tried to serialize the response, it failed: TypeError: 'ObjectId' object is not iterable. Mitigation: one-line addition in get_current_user — `if user.get('created_by'): user['created_by'] = str(user['created_by'])`. Verified by re-running pytest (9/9 pass including test_new_admin_can_login).
